sections.paragraph:
to cover, to cover over; kindred roots כָּסָא and כָּשָׂה. (Syr. ܟܣܐܳ id. also to put on; Arab. كسا to put on; Ch. כְּסָה to cover; hence, to be hidden, concealed.) In Kal it is only found in part. כֹּסֶה Pro 12:16, 23 Pro 12:23, and כָּסוּי Psa 32:1. Much more frequently used is-

Piel כִּסָּה.-

(1) to cover, followed by an acc. Exo 10:5 Num 9:15 22:5 , or by עַל (like other verbs of covering סָכַךְ, כָּפַר), as if eine Decke machen über etwas; Num 16:33, וַתְּכַס עֲלֵיהֶם הָאָרֶץ “and the earth covered them,” prop. covered over them; Job 21:26, וְרִמָּה תְּכַסֶּה עֲלֵיהֶם “and the worms cover them;” 2Ch 5:8 followed by לְ Isa 11:9. To cover some person, or thing with any thing, const.-(a) followed by an acc. of pers. and בְּ of the covering; Levit. 17:13 , וְכִסָּהוּ בֶּעָפָר “and he shall cover it (the blood) with dust;” Num 4:5, , 11 .-(b) followed by two acc. Eze 18:7, 16 Eze 18:16 16:10 .-(c) followed by עַל of pers. and בְּ of the covering, Psa 44:20.-(d) with an acc. of the covering, and עַל of the thing to be covered, Eze 24:7 comp. Job 36:32. Often used metaph. to cover over sin, i.e. to pardon; followed by an acc. Psa 85:3 followed by עַל Pro 10:12 Neh. 3:37 Psa 32:1 [Kal]; כְּסוּי חַטָּאָה “whose sin is covered,” i.e. forgiven; compare כָּפַר. כִּסָּה אֶלּ pregn. used for to confide covertly in any one, Psa 143:9.

(2) Intrans. to cover oneself, to put on any thing, Gen 38:14 Deu 22:12 Jon 3:6, וַיְכַס שַׂק “and he clothed himself with sackcloth.” (Arabic كَسَا to put on; followed by an acc.)

(3) to cover, to conceal (compare Chald.), Pro 10:18 12:16 , 23 Pro 12:23[Kal]; Job 31:33. Job 23:17, וּמִפָּנַי כִּסָּה־אֹפֶל “and (because) he hath (not) covered the darkness from my sight;” has not set me free from calamities.

Pual כֻּסָּה and כָּסָּה ( Psa 80:11 Pro 24:31); pass. to be covered; followed by בְּ of the covering, 1Ch 21:16 Ecc 6:4 also by an acc. Psa 80:11, כָּסּוּ חָרִים צִלָּהּ “the mountains were covered with its shadow.” Pro 24:31.

Niphal, Jer 51:42 Eze 24:8.

Hithpael, to cover over oneself; followed by בְּ of covering, Isa 59:6 1Ki 11:29 once followed by an acc. Jon 3:8.

Derived nouns, כָּסוּי, כְּסוּת (and סוּת), כֶּסֶת, מִכְסֶה, מְכַסֶּה.
